hero.title: JD Tailoring
hero.description: The tailoring workflow is where structured resume data becomes job-specific delivery. The page now describes how the product should behave once the AI service is connected.

items.title: Capture the job target
items.description: Collect the role title, company, seniority expectation, and the full JD body.

items.title: Analyze the source resume
items.description: Identify which sections are strongest for the target role and which ones should move up or be softened.

items.title: Rewrite with guardrails
items.description: Improve phrasing, ordering, and emphasis while preserving facts and keeping the template stable.

items.title: Review before export
items.description: Show the user what changed and let them approve the final version before delivery.

engine.title: Execution baseline
engine.description: These values describe the current technical baseline already prepared in the project.

rules.title: Tailoring rules
rules.description: These rules should become the default system behavior.

items.title: Rewrite, do not fabricate
items.description: AI can sharpen language and re-order emphasis, but it should not create outcomes, metrics, or roles that are not present in the source resume.

items.title: Prefer section-level control
items.description: The workflow should work on structured sections and bullet points so version review stays predictable.

items.title: Explain why each change exists
items.description: Every major rewrite should tie back to the job title or a concrete requirement in the JD.

items.title: Keep export compatibility
items.description: Any tailored content must still fit the fixed template when rendered back to DOCX and PDF.

deliverables.title: Expected outputs
deliverables.description: A good tailoring run should produce more than a single rewritten paragraph.

items.title: Match summary
items.description: A concise explanation of how the current resume aligns with the target role.

items.title: Section rewrite suggestions
items.description: What to rewrite in profile, experience, projects, skills, and strengths.

items.title: Export-ready version
items.description: A final structured version that is safe to render into the fixed template.

composer.title: Run a tailoring pass
composer.description: Pick a parsed resume, provide the target role and full JD, and the system rewrites the structured content within the factual guardrails.
composer.role_placeholder: Example: AI Product Manager / Data Product Manager
composer.jd_placeholder: Paste the full JD including responsibilities, requirements, keywords, and bonus signals.
composer.empty_description: Upload and parse a resume in the studio before you run JD tailoring.

preview.title: Current tailoring result
preview.base_summary_empty: This resume does not have a summary yet.
preview.highlights_empty: No fit highlights yet. After a tailoring run, this area will show the strongest JD-aligned signals.
preview.changes_empty: No changes tracked yet. After a tailoring run, this area will show section-level reasons and before/after text.
